topic: the Halifax explosion 1917 — Vince Coleman's last telegram

candidates.title: Halifax Waterfront 1917 (Library of Congress)
candidates.date_confidence_reason: LOC catalog dates this 1917 — pre-explosion waterfront setting
candidates.suggested_use: Establish Halifax Harbour pre-explosion — quiet, working waterfront
candidates.notes: Smaller resolution; use as quick setup shot. Ken Burns: slow zoom-in

candidates.title: Halifax, NS, looking north from grain elevator toward Acadia Sugar Refinery, ca. 1900
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Pre-1917 by ~17 years — same harbour, useful for 'spectators' beat as wide city view
candidates.suggested_use: Wide view of pre-explosion Halifax — the city the spectators are about to lose
candidates.notes: Era-mismatch flag: 1900 not 1917, but same geography. Ken Burns: slow pan-right

candidates.title: Blast cloud from the Halifax Explosion, December 6, 1917
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Wikimedia Information template specifies the exact day of the explosion
candidates.suggested_use: THE iconic blast cloud shot — the moment of detonation
candidates.notes: Horizontal aspect. Ken Burns: slow zoom-in on the cloud's base

candidates.title: Halifax Explosion blast cloud (vertical)
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Day-of explosion image, period-authentic vertical framing
candidates.suggested_use: VERTICAL blast cloud — ideal for 9:16 framing without crop
candidates.notes: Native 9:16-ish aspect ratio. Ken Burns: slow zoom-out from cloud base reveals scale

candidates.title: Halifax Explosion blast cloud (restored vertical)
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Restoration of the iconic vertical blast cloud photograph
candidates.suggested_use: Higher-clarity restored version — recommend over halifax-004 if equal coverage
candidates.notes: Same image as halifax-004 but cleaned up. Use one or the other, not both

candidates.title: Halifax Explosion — harbour view (restored)
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Days-after the explosion — same harbor where Coleman worked
candidates.suggested_use: Harbor immediately after — could serve as transition into aftermath
candidates.notes: High res, restored. Ken Burns: pan-right across the wrecked harbour

candidates.archive_id: Halifax after Halifax Explosion looking toward Dartmouth 1917
candidates.title: Halifax after the Halifax Explosion, looking toward Dartmouth, 1917
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Wikimedia dates this 'after December 6, 1917' — direct aftermath shot
candidates.suggested_use: Dartmouth shore — same direction the 18m wave traveled to wipe out Tufts Cove
candidates.notes: Strong fit for the tsunami beat. Ken Burns: slow pan-left across the wave's path

candidates.title: Halifax Explosion Aftermath (Library of Congress, restored)
candidates.suggested_use: Devastated street scene — windows shattered everywhere
candidates.notes: Lower resolution (960x681) — flag for visible upscaling. Use for shorter dwell

candidates.archive_id: Looking North toward Pier 8 from Hillis Foundry
candidates.title: Looking North toward Pier 8 from Hillis Foundry after the Halifax Explosion, ca. 1917
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Pier 8 = adjacent to Pier 6 where Mont-Blanc exploded — direct ground-zero view
candidates.suggested_use: Ground zero — twisted iron, leveled buildings
candidates.notes: Wide aspect — will need significant crop for 9:16. Ken Burns: zoom-in on a central detail

candidates.title: Temporary hospital after Halifax explosion (1)
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Dated to December 1917 — days after the explosion
candidates.suggested_use: Hospital — visceral payoff for the eye-injury line (249 enucleations performed)
candidates.notes: Borderline resolution. Ken Burns: slow zoom-in on a single bed/face if visible

candidates.title: Temporary hospital after Halifax explosion (2)
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Dated to December 1917 — same series as halifax-010
candidates.suggested_use: Hospital — companion to halifax-010, second angle

candidates.archive_id: 19171208 Halifax explosion map Boston Daily Globe
candidates.title: Halifax explosion with map, The Boston Daily Globe, Dec 8 1917
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Newspaper dated 1917-12-08 — two days after the explosion
candidates.suggested_use: Period newspaper — sets up the era and the way the world heard the news
candidates.notes: Optional flourish. Ken Burns: slow pan/zoom-in on the headline

candidates.archive_id: Panoramic view of damage Halifax waterfront 1917
candidates.title: Panoramic view of damage to Halifax waterfront after the Halifax Explosion, 1917
candidates.date_confidence_reason: Wikimedia dates to the day of the explosion
candidates.suggested_use: Closing wide shot — the full scope of devastation across the waterfront
candidates.notes: VERY wide aspect (6.2:1) — works only as a pan across a crop, not a single 9:16 frame

risks: No verified photograph of Vince Coleman himself in Wikimedia — Reel anchors visually on the harbor, the cloud, and the aftermath rather than his face.
No specific photograph of Tufts Cove pre- or post-tsunami. The tsunami beat uses halifax-007 (Dartmouth-side view).
Two of the strongest aftermath shots (halifax-008 at 960x681, halifax-009 at 2700x1069) require either upscaling or crop. Flag at assembly.
Mont-Blanc and Imo searches returned 0 results — no ship photos available. Reel doesn't visually show the ships; this fits the script (script never names them).
